gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found that explicit release probe JSON paths could alias planned debug probe outputs. I added canonical path validation to reject those aliases, covered planned-debug and planned-native alias cases, reran focused/full tests and build, and re-review returned no findings.
verification: poetry run pytest -q tests/test_benchmark_plan.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py (68 passed)
GPT-5.5 review found explicit release probe path aliasing planned debug outputs; fixed with canonical-path guard and re-review returned no findings
what_changed: Added benchmark-plan validation that prevents release evidence from consuming planned debug engine probes with engine_version overrides or non-native debug mode.
Rejected explicit --release-engine-probe-json paths that alias planned debug probe outputs while preserving explicit paths that alias planned native probe outputs.
Documented the planned-probe release-safety rule and added CLI regressions for rejected debug plans, explicit native artifacts, and alias handling.
why: The generated V1 release plan should not be able to produce debug/non-native engine-probe records and then validate them as release evidence by accident or path alias.
